info.title: FieldOps API
info.description: The CNH FieldsOps API provides access to to agronomic data and vehicle telemetry for both agronomic machinery and construction equipment connected to a FieldOps account. The secure RESTful API endpoints can be used for Farm Management and Operation Centres. To learn more and access, visit the [CNH Developer Portal](https://develop.cnh.com)

tags.name: Tokens
tags.description: To Authenticate User and to get the access and Refresh Token Related APIs.

get.description: Get Authorization code

post.description:
This resource processes requests for both access tokens and refresh tokens based on the specified grant_type:

- **`grant_type`**: This request body parameter is required to obtain an access token and refresh token.
  - To retrieve an access token and refresh token, the `grant_type` value must be `authorization_code`.
    - Additionally, the `code` parameter is required when `grant_type` is set to `authorization_code` and the value must be the **code** received during the authorization process.
  - To obtain an access token using a refresh token, the `grant_type` value must be `refresh_token`.
    - Additionally, the `refresh_token` parameter is required when `grant_type` is set to `refresh_token` and the value must be the **refresh token** received during the authorization process.

get.description: Get configuration information about the OpenID Connect provider.

The Fleet snapshot provides a list of equipments for the account and data elements as of their indicated date and time.
For any changes in the fleet of vehicles, there may be a delay of up to one hour.
**Note**: CumulativeIdleHours and CumulativeNonProductiveIdleHours are only available for the last seven days.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.

The Equipment snapshot provides data elements for a single equipment as of the indicated date and time.

**Note**: CumulativeIdleHours and CumulativeNonProductiveIdleHours are only available for the last seven days.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.
This resource will retrieves a complete list of equipment, along with identifying information, for the company.
This resource will retrieves equipment by VIN, including brand and model.

The Location Time Series call displays the location of the specified equipment within the date range provided.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.

The Distance Time Series call pulls a list of odometer readings for the specified equipment in the date range provided. There could be odometer readings for every 15 minutes the vehicle traveled during the time specified.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.

The Cumulative Operating Hours Time Series call will display the total lifetime hours that the equipment was in work status. 

**Note** :Default page size is 100.

The Cumulative Fuel Used Time Series call will display the total amount of fuel used by the specified equipment.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.

The Cumulative Idle Hours Time Series call will display the total number of idle hours a equipment.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.

The Diesel Exhaust Fluid (DEF) Remaining Time Series call will display the percentage of DEF remaining for a specified equipment.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.

The Fuel Remaining Ratio Time Series call will display the percentage of fuel remaining for a specified equipment.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.

The Caution Codes Time Series call will display a list of alarms for a specified equipment during the date range provided.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.

parameters.name: verbose
parameters.description: Allows non ISO specified parameters in the response if verbose is 'true'

get.description:
The Engine Condition Time Series call will display a true or false value to indicate if the specified equipment�s engine is currently running.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.

The Peak Daily Speed call will display the highest ground speed a specified equipment reached in the last 24 hours for each day during the date range provided.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.

get.summary: Cumulative Non Productive Idle Hours Time Series.
get.description:
The Cumulative Non Productive Idle Hours call will display the total hours the engine was running, but the equipment was not moving and the equipment controls were not operated.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.

get.summary: Fuel Used In The Preceding 24Hours Time Series.
get.description:
The Fuel Used in the Preceding 24 hours call will display the quantity of fuel a specific equipment has used in the previous 24 hours for each day during the date range provided.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.

The Diagnostic Trouble Code Time Series call will display a list of diagnostic trouble Codes that alert the owner for  non-scheduled equipment support.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.

get.summary: Average Daily Engine Load Factor Time Series
get.description:
The Average Daily Engine Load Factor provides the percentage of time that the machine was working for each day during the specified date range.

**Note** :Default page size is 100.
The Metrics call will display available metrics for a specific equipment within the date range provided.

get.description:
Retrieves all operationsbyvehicle for the specified company.
  • **action** - This field will be included in the API response only when the ETag header is provided
  • **pagination** - This field will be excluded from the API response when the ETag header is present
  • **VIN** - This field will not be included in the API response if it contains null values.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Excludes results dated before the provided value

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Excludes results dated after the provided value

  • **VIN** - This field will not be included in the API response if it contains null values.

Get all OperationsByvehicle for the given field for the specified company.
.
  • **action** - This field will be included in the API response only when the ETag header is provided
  • **pagination** - This field will be excluded from the API response when the ETag header is present
  • **VIN** - This field will not be included in the API response if it contains null values.

post.description:
This request is to upload a Data File to FieldOps account. The file should be a .zip or .jdp file(for Raven job data file). This request will return a fileId which can be used to check the file status. Only a single file can be uploaded at a time.
  • Queued (Trimble and Raven Files) - Queued for auto Processing
  • Uploaded (CN1, ISOXML and John Deere) - Uploaded and waiting for manual process
  • Processing
  • Complete - File processed successfully
  • Failed - Failed to process the file.

Get a list of product mixes - the baseSolutionUOMId represents a unit of volume for liquid forms and a unit of weight for solid mixes. The baseSolutionRate is a number in baseSolutionUOM/square meter.

post.description:
This request is used to send a prescription file directly to a machine belonging to the given company. The response from this endpoint will contain a requestID which can be used to see the status of the request.
The included file should be a zip file containing shapefile files (.shp, .shx, and .dbf).
If the activityTypeId indicates a tillage job, productId and areaUOMId must be null, otherwise they are required.

parameters.name: VIN
parameters.description: The vehicle identification number (not the guid)

get.description:
To get the status of the prescription sent to the vehicle in the POST prescriptionFile, use the requestId in the GET perscriptionFileRequest call.

Statuses:

  • Scheduled
  • DownloadingFile
  • CreatingPrescription
  • ExportingFile
  • SendingFile
  • WaitingForVehicle
  • Complete
  • Failed.

EquipmentID.description:
The Equipment ID identifies the end user’s name for an individual asset.
The end user will provide this information to the telematics provider during fleet registration.

SerialNumber.description:
Serial Number.

*The asset tracker IMEI value should be used as the serial number.*

VIN.description:
Vehicle Identification Number.

The VIN shall be as defined by ISO 3779 and is used for on-road vehicles.

fileType.description:
A uniquie value indicating supported file types. Below are the supported file types
  • AGGPS - Trimble
  • ISOXML - ISO 11783
  • RAV-ADV - Raven
  • RAV-JDP - Raven Job Data File
  • RAV-ZIP - Raven Job Data File
  • RCD_GS4 - John Deere Gen 4
  • RCD - John Deere
  • CN1 - CaseIH/New Holland/Steyr

file.description: The prescription file to send to the vehicle. It should be a zip file containing shapefile files (.shp, .shx, and .dbf).

fieldId.description: A uuid indicating the field the prescription belongs to

seasonId.description: A uuid representing the season this prescription applies to

activityTypeId.description: A uuid indicating what kind of activity this prescription is for (e.g. seeding, spraying, tillage, fertilizing)

productId.description: The product being applied, must be null if the activity is tillage

activityUOMId.description: The activity based unit of measurement - for seeding this can be seed or weight, for tillage it is a lengh, for fertilizer and spraying, it can be weight or length

areaUOMId.description: The unit of measure for areas (e.g. acres, hectares), must be null if the activity is tillage

shapeFileAttributeName.description: The target rate attribute controlled by the prescription, i.e. TGT_RATE.

client_id.description: The unique identifier for the client application.

client_secret.description: The secret key for the client application, used for authentication.

grant_type.description:
- **`grant_type`**: This request body parameter is required to obtain an access token and refresh token.
  - To retrieve an access token and refresh token, the `grant_type` value must be `authorization_code`.
    - Additionally, the `code` parameter is required when `grant_type` is set to `authorization_code`, and the value must be the **code** received during the authorization process.
  - To obtain an access token using a refresh token, the `grant_type` value must be `refresh_token`.
    - Additionally, the `refresh_token` parameter is required when `grant_type` is set to `refresh_token`, and the value must be the **refresh token** received during the authorization process.

redirect_uri.description: The URI where the authorization server sends the user after authorization.

code.description: The authorization code received from the authorization server after user consent.

oneOf.description: Request payload for the `authorization_code` grant type.

client_id.description: The unique identifier for the client application.

client_secret.description: The secret key for the client application, used for authentication.

redirect_uri.description: The URI where the authorization server sends the user after authorization.

refresh_token.description: The refresh token used to obtain a new access token (required for `refresh_token` grant

oneOf.description: Request payload for the `refresh_token` grant type.

range.name: Range
range.description: Range of bytes to download from a file.  Up to 20 megabytes may be requested at a time. Example; bytes=0-20971520

languages.name: language
languages.description:
The desired language(s) for text translations provided as a comma separated list e.g. de,es

Currently supported languages are:
ar, bg, hr, cs, da, nl, et, fi, fr, de, el, jaHira, hu, it, ja, lv, lt, zh, no, pl, pt, pt-BR, ro, ru, sr, sk, sl, es, es-MX, es-419, sv, th, tr, he, fr-CA, ko, uk, en

cursor.name: cursor
cursor.description: A cursor used for pagination on large responses

limit.name: limit
limit.description: limits the number of records shown on a page
